name: P0: AssistantPanel voice control exposes listening or clear error
description: Logged-in user opens the docked AI chat, verifies the voice mic control is present, clicks it, and either enters Listening state or sees a clear unsupported/permission/failed message. Typed input remains usable afterward. Does not require real speech transcription in the cloud browser.

planSteps.description: Type demo@timeora.app into the email field
Type TimeoraDemo123! into the password field
Click the Sign in button and wait up to 8 seconds for the dashboard weekly calendar to load
If the language toggle shows id, switch it to English (en) so labels match Start voice input / Stop voice input
Open the AI calendar chat by clicking Open AI chat or pressing Control+K / Meta+K
Wait for 2 seconds for the Ask your calendar panel to open
Verify the assistant composer shows a voice button with accessible name or title Start voice input (mic control near the message input)
Click the Start voice input button and wait 3 seconds

Verify either: (A) listening UI appears with Listening or Stop voice input, or (B) a clear error message is shown such as Browser does not support voice input, Microphone permission denied, No speech detected, or Voice input failed — without crashing the panel
If listening is active, click Stop voice input and wait 1 second
Click the assistant message input and type What do I have today? without submitting yet
Verify the typed text What do I have today? is visible in the input, proving voice interaction did not break keyboard input
Click Send or press Enter and wait up to 6 seconds for an assistant reply
Verify the assistant returns a schedule-related response message (events list, empty day, or help) so the chat path still works after voice interaction
